title: UK's First Commercial Biomethanol Bunkering Service Launches at Port of Immingham
content: Exolum, Methanex, and Ã˜rsted have launched the UK's first commercial biomethanol bunkering service at the Port of Immingham. This initiative leverages existing infrastructure to supply biomethanol, a cleaner fuel capable of reducing greenhouse gas emissions by up to 80% compared to conventional marine fuels. Under the partnership, Exolum provides the storage and fueling infrastructure, while Methanex supplies the biomethanol. Ã˜rsted will be the flagship customer, utilizing the fuel for its offshore wind maintenance vessels in the North Sea. The launch proceeds despite the International Maritime Organization (IMO) delaying its Net Zero Framework vote, underscoring the partners' commitment to decarbonizing the maritime sector. Domestic shipping currently accounts for 4.7% of UK transport emissions, and this project establishes a scalable supply chain to help address both local and international shipping footprints.
author: Analysts Account
